channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Two Years On, 20 Nigerian States Yet to Fully Implement ₦70,000 Minimum Wage
item.description: E don nearly two years since President Bola Ahmed Tinubu sign the ₦70,000 minimum wage into law for Nigerian workers back in July 2024, but up till now, about 20 states still never pay the full amount...

item.title: Over 200 people arrested during Palestine Action demonstration in central London
item.description: More than 200 individuals were arrested by the Metropolitan Police during a protest organised by Palestine Action in central London. The demonstration, which took place near the Israeli Embassy and ot...

item.title: How the U.S. War in Iran Affected Its Global Influence and Strategic Goals
item.description: The recent conflict between the United States and Iran, culminating in a 14-day ceasefire, has had significant repercussions for America's global standing and strategic priorities. While U.S. policyma...

item.title: Updated Braddon hotel plans reduce building bulk in response to community concerns
item.description: Developer Nik Bulum's latest designs for the Braddon Hotel, on the former Canberra City Bowling Club site, have been released for public consideration after over a decade of debate. The new plans redu...

item.title: Tech Executive Suggests Exit Tax on Canadians Who Emigrate After Education
item.description: At the federal Liberal Party convention, Patrick Pichette, a Canadian who built his career at Microsoft and Google before moving to Europe, suggested imposing a $500,000 exit tax on Canadians leaving ...

item.title: BINI’s Sheena honors late mother after Coachella performance debut
item.description: BINI member Sheena Catacutan took a heartfelt moment to dedicate her group’s milestone appearance at Coachella 2026 to her late mother, Angelyn. In an emotional post following the group’s performance ...

item.title: Ian Baker-Finch raises concerns over LIV golfers’ preparation for major championships
item.description: Former British Open champion Ian Baker-Finch has expressed doubts about whether LIV Golf tournaments adequately prepare players for the challenges of major championships, following a mixed performance...

item.title: Ontario Vineyards Face Potential Winter Damage Amid Growing Wine Industry
item.description: Ontario grape growers are assessing the impact of one of the coldest and most persistent winters in recent memory, which has threatened local vineyards. While the full extent of the damage is still un...

item.title: Former Detective Convicted of Corruption in 2018 Murder Case
item.description: The Pinetown Regional Court is set to sentence former detective Bonginkosi Dlamini, convicted of accepting a R120,000 bribe to manipulate evidence in the 2018 murder of ANC branch leader Thulani Nxuma...

item.title: Ghana moves to ease fuel costs by temporarily reducing taxes amid global oil price surge
item.description: The Government of Ghana has announced plans to temporarily suspend a number of fuel taxes and levies to cushion citizens from the recent sharp increase in pump prices. The move comes as global oil pri...

item.title: Meralco Bolts extend winning run against Macau in PBA Commissioner’s Cup
item.description: The Meralco Bolts continued their strong showing in the PBA Commissioner’s Cup, securing another decisive win against the Macau Black Knights. The Bolts dominated both ends of the court, demonstrating...

item.title: Tyson Fury Returns to the Ring Against Arslanbek Makhmudov After 16-Month Break
item.description: Tyson Fury made his much-anticipated return to the boxing ring after a 16-month hiatus, facing off against Russia’s Arslanbek Makhmudov at Tottenham Hotspur Stadium in London. This bout marked Fury’s ...

item.title: How grief and curiosity shape MasterChef Australia judge Sofia Levin
item.description: This feature profiles MasterChef Australia judge and food writer Sofia Levin, exploring how her love of eating and her experience of personal loss shape the way she works and lives. Levin is portrayed...

item.title: Man Killed in Mariannhill Shooting on M1 Higginson Highway
item.description: On Saturday afternoon, police and paramedics responded to a shooting incident on the M1 Higginson Highway in Mariannhill, just outside Pinetown. ALS Paramedics reported that they arrived shortly befor...

item.title: Burnley struggle to avoid relegation after loss to Brighton
item.description: Burnley don land for serious wahala as dem dey face relegation back to the Championship after dem lose 2-0 to Brighton for Turf Moor on Saturday. Since October, dem don only win one match for league, ...

item.title: Kenyan Woman Finds Comfort Lying on Mother's Grave, Shares Emotional Video
item.description: A video circulating online has captured the touching way a Kenyan woman, Love Ngajiro, is grieving the loss of her mother. The video shows Ngajiro lying on her mother's beautifully tiled grave, a gest...

item.title: MK Party MP Visvin Reddy fined for inciting public violence during 2024 election campaign
item.description: The Durban regional court has sentenced MK Party MP Visvin Reddy to a R30,000 fine or 10 months’ imprisonment, with half of the sentence suspended for five years, following his guilty plea to inciting...

item.title: First Great Indian Bustard chick born in Gujarat through conservation egg transfer
item.description: Gujarat has witnessed a remarkable milestone in wildlife conservation as the first Great Indian Bustard (GIB) chick hatched after a decade-long effort. The Union Environment Ministry announced on Marc...

item.title: Assessing Australia’s Economic Approach: Subsidies, Industry Protection and Energy Challenges
item.description: Australia is facing economic pressures amid disrupted global oil and gas supplies, which are likely to persist well beyond the resolution of ongoing conflicts in the Middle East. The country’s respons...

item.title: Ottawa Senators move closer to securing NHL playoff berth with win over Islanders
item.description: The Ottawa Senators delivered a strong performance on Saturday, defeating the New York Islanders 3-0 and moving closer to securing a spot in the NHL playoffs. Ridly Greig scored a short-handed goal at...
